reviews.title: Best gift ever
reviews.text: Bought this for a 9 year old. It’s amazing. It has so many things on it and is easy to use. She shows it to everyone who will look at it and she feels like it’s just like her big brothers and sisters watches. Very happy.

reviews.title: 2G is Dead
reviews.text: This watch says that it utilizes a sim card with 2g service. This service is nearly dead everywhere. The sim card area in the watch is a little tricky to figure out. You have to open the back and then lift up the battery slightly. The sim card slot is located under the battery. You have to slide(left/right) and lift up the metal sim card cover like a door. Insert the sim card and lay the metal cover down over it and slide it into place to lock it. This watch is not waterproof. The watch works as specified except for the 2g calling. 2g service is not available in my area, so make sure you check if your area still has it. You have to buy your own sim card and they recommend Speedtalk service. Check their coverage map for 2g service.

reviews.title: So comfortable!
reviews.text: Best band I’ve ever worn! These are so comfortable and lightweight that I sometimes forgot I have my Fitbit on! It’s breathable! I like that it was so many holes that you can use to tighten with. Sometimes with others their so far apart that it’s righter too loose or too tight. Trust me, you will not have that issue with this band! The design is so beautiful and the colors are perfect!

reviews.title: Great bands
reviews.text: Comfortable and lots of holes to change size. Looks pretty and I like the thinner band.

reviews.title: Easier to set-up than previous bands
reviews.text:
I have really struggled getting replacement bands in place in the past. I had this one switched over in a matter of minutes. The lacy look makes it less apparent that the band is vinyl and the dark colors help to hide stains. I have bad metal allergies nd was a bit afraid of what would happen with the clasp given it is metal and has some contact with my skin. Been wearing this since December and the only time I have issues with a rash was when I had the watch fastened too tightly to my wrist. I believe this was from trapped sweat and moisture and not a metal allergy reaction.
Extra great deal in that you get three bands. I don't foresee a need to buy more bands for at least a year.

reviews.title: Angry customer

It wouldn't even turn on right away after charging! The headphones are absolutely the worst thing ever ! I plugged them in to my phone and all we heard was high pitched screeching!  I'm not asking for my money back, I'm just warning others to be careful!  Complete false advertising!

reviews.title: Not a Good Choice for Me
reviews.text:
Like - was able to call and receive texts. Was able to answer a call on the watch. Pairing was easy. The app was easy.
Dislike - Once paired, the watch takes all your media volume away from your phone. You won't hear any music like from YouTube, text message alert or phone ringing. The watch has a little vibration that you can barely feel, when you receive a text or getting a phone call. All sounds like from Facebook videos or anything, goes through the watch. The only way I could find to get it to quit doing this, was to unpair the watch totally. I need military time but couldn't find a way to make the time in a 24 hour format. You have to put the watch together. The bands come apart from the watch. Everytime you move your hand the watch face lights up. This was definitely not what I was looking for at all. Would I buy this again? No.

reviews.title: Nice resolution
reviews.text:
I bought this watch because of the high-resolution display. And that's what I got, no more, no less.

Upon first impression, I can tell the display has crisp graphics. The touch screen is responsive. The processor is fast. Overall, the phone itself is nice.

The app, on the other hand, is awful. It is intuitive, but it loses Bluetooth connectivity without reason. This is the only app that I've seen that will move forward and backward during a dial sync. When I switch to certain dials, the progress bar will reach 61%, then count back down to 60%, then 59%, then start going back up. It's nice to see honesty in an app, but it's just strange.

I didn't test notifications from other apps, but no SMS text messages came through. The setup is simple and easy with SMS notifications settings on its own section. But once set up, no messages came through.

Battery life? What battery life? After a fully charge, this watch drained to 90% in the 15 minutes I used to overlook the features and set up the app. This from a 330mAh battery. At this rate, the battery will be depleted in less than two hours.

reviews.title: Junk
reviews.text: What a piece of junk. The instructions don't match the watch or the Amazon description and is written in awful broken English. And the app--- AWFUL. Navigation is bad, the English on the app is even worse than the instructions. I'm seriously concerned that downloading the app has somehow compromised anything on my phone. While I understand Amazon is a gigantic company that can't police every single item for sale, this is just BLATANTLY bad. In case my review isn't already clear, DO NOT BUY.

reviews.title: Great watch for the price
reviews.text: It was very difficult for me to set up this watch until I scanned the scan code from the pamphlet. The directions weren't easy. I think I would like a watch a little smaller but I like the shape and bands.  The price was great. I'm still not sure about the sleep mode, nothing seems to be registering. Steps are showing up which is cool, BPM is registering. Still figuring it out. Arrived promptly.  Additional info: the watch is NOT tracking my erratic sleep pattern, which I really needed it to do. I also found the size of the round watch intrusive to my long sleeve shirts and arm. Loved the pink and green bands and the color of the watch face. Steps are also not being accurately recorded. So sad, really had high hopes for this.

reviews.title: Worth the money n saved my life
reviews.text: Love the feminine color. Easy to see. Helps me keep track of certain health irregularities as they happen therefore possibly averting a health emergency. Ty for having an ingenious machine in such a lovely and compact device.

reviews.title: Like it was made by Fitbit!
reviews.text:
I love this strap it is so pretty and such a value for the money. It looks like it cost way more. The little kit it came with to attach it to the Fitbit and to remove extra links was so handy and easy to use. It attached with no trouble at all.

The only issue I had with it was that the rose gold color is slightly off from the Fitbit, but when wearing it, it’s not next to it directly so you can’t really tell. It’s not enough to bother me or take a star off but it could be for some.

reviews.title: Surprised at the comfort and beauty of the band.
reviews.text:
I've only had it on for a few hours, but I love it. The colors, black, pink on the resin look so rich and shiny. And it's a comfortable fit. I did have to take out 2 tabs to get a snug fit. Taking out the tabs wasn't difficult after figuring out the way the tool that came with it. Can't say the instructions were the best, but I figured it out.
I always got the magnetic mesh band in rose gold, but it always discolored and no matter what i tried I couldn't get it cleaned.
This is why I tried a new band. A review prompt gave suggestions of different things to comment on. One of the suggestions was a mobile app. I have no idea what that had to do with the band. Maybe someone knows and can let me know.
UPDATE:
I've been wearing it 24 hours a day, except when showering or recharging the watch. NO discloration, no running of colors (bracelet) and it doesn't get dirty looking like the metalic bands. I'm going to buy another one, just in case something happens to this. So worth the money.

⌚50M Waterproof /Extremely resistant to damage - Incredibly strong and corrosion-resistant titanium case has been used to enclose and giving cover, the rugged military grade smart watch with fully enclosed ultra-thin case design can be easily submerged into 50M (IP69K waterproof) deep water. ⌚IndustryLeading dual smart chip - ① AI Voice Assistant & Make Calls: A very handy tool built in the watch. Use your voice to give instructions and the voice assistant will do the rest. You can also make voice calls through the voice assistant, makes the scenes that are inconvenient to hold a mobile phone in daily exercise and life more concise and smart. ② Intelligent sports recognition: features BioTracker PPG optical sensor and the advanced AI algorithm,M2 enables smart recognition of 6 sports. The system can automatically sense current sports status without entering Sports Modes manually.

⌚60+ Days super battery life - M2 is equipped with a 100% pure cobalt battery (military grade), the battery capacity is as high as 380mAh ensuring optimal performance and longevity which is twice as much as that of conventional smart watches. At the same time, super power-saving AI algorithm breaking the industry is adopted to minimize power consumption, enough to last up to 2 weeks under normal use, and can continue to use sports mode, health index measurement and other functions for up to 60+ days in power-saving mode. With 30-minute charge, you can have enough battery backup to last for up to 3 days.

⌚24H Health monitoring /70 Exercise modes - AMAZTIM M2 collects the health data with its advanced real-time monitoring function. The fitness watch helps you monitor All-day sleep, 24-hour heart rate, and real-time blood pressure and blood oxygen. The fitness tracking watch includes a pedometer to keep track of the calories burned throughout the day. Supports up to 70 sports modes including indoor and outdoor exercises such as hiking, skiing, swimming, etc. The smartwatches collects the data with pinpoint precision and accuracy, ensuring that every workout is counted.

reviews.title: Easy to set up, very accurate, keeps me updated with many aspect of my health
reviews.text:
I really appreciate this watch and the features it has. First off, the smart watch was very easy to pair to my phone. I have an Android phone, and it only took a few minutes to pair the watch with my phone.
First enable Bluetooth and find the device listed as "C20"

Next, download the app that's shown in the QR code. Once you have the watch paired via Bluetooth to your smart phone and the app is downloaded, your watch ready to go, and should be syncing to your smart phone. The connection has been great thus far; it hasn't disconnected/lost connection to my phone.

As someone who has suffers from pulmonary lung disease, I was very excited when I found out this smart watch has the capability to track my oxygen saturation, heart rate, and blood pressure. In addition, you don't have to do anything, the watch will automatically to measurements periodically throughout the day.

It's helping me monitor and compare results over a few days. I suggest authorizing Google Fit so your hearth history is accessible when it. For me, it helps to have a history when I meet with my pulmonologist.

The watch has great standby battery life, and it's light weight. After m three days it's only dipped minimally in available battery percentage. I highly recommend this!

**Updated**

So it's been several days now since I first posted the review and I am amazed how little the battery has drained. Needless to say this smartwatch has fantastic standby battery life; especially considering it's constantly looking for/measuring heart rate. As you can see from the photos the readings are very accurate. I own a pulse oximeter and a blood pressure monitor to keep my pulmonary hypertension in check. My resting heart rate is 95 bpm, which may seem high, but the reading is accurate and normal which makes this product more the reassuring.

reviews.title: Great Watch!
reviews.text: Ok, I have had fitness watches from Samsung to Fitbit to Garmin and this one is the best....especially for the price.  First thing since turning 60 I need a watch I can read in the sunlight.  This has a brightness level I actually do not have to turn it all the way up and the workout data is big enough to read. I think all the health data is correct, but do not trust the Blood Pressure readings because of the science behind it. Actually do not trust any BP on a fitness watch.  There is a lot of customization you can do......I recommend a custom watch face along with setting up quick view on a schedule. I am now going to see if I can get a spare charging cord,

reviews.title: Cheap, Inaccurate, but Cheap
reviews.text:
What works, the clock.
What doesn't work, everything else.
Bought in August, really tried to give it a chance. I knew for the price it was a gamble.

reviews.title: Step count
reviews.text:
Like the look, heft, easy set up.

But I question the accuracy since driving 1 miles and it's counted 30 steps, this is AFTER it logged 82 steps during at my desk.

3 month update. Battery life is still excellent however the watch has stopped connecting to the app

6 month update: Batter life is excellent, I notice it has started to decline, but that could also be heavier use. Step count still seems a bit wonky, but not egregiously off. The Strap is starting to break at the clasp and I will need to replace as soon as I'm able to find a replacement.

reviews.title: Nice
reviews.text: Having used different smart watches was looking forward to this one. But it disappointed me. Accuracy on everything was off compared to reality. Other smart watches for less were more accurate. I returned said watch.

reviews.title: Great watch for a great price
reviews.text: I was skeptical about the watch as I had purchased other watches only to have them fail miserably. This watch turned out great. The most important thing to do is read the manual. Especially about how to wear the watch, which is a little higher than a normal watch. This will make the accuracy much better. The step counter is controlled by arm movement not actual steps, so if you walk stiff armed , it will be inaccurate. Move your arms when walking , the watch will be much more accurate.Make sure you go into the app on your phone and activate the exercises your interested in. This will help the watch to track it better. Also activate the media part of the app for sms,email etc. You can also monitor who is calling you, so you can choose to answer you phone or not. I am extremely pleased with my purchase and will be watching for other products from this company.

reviews.title: Some Good Aspects, But Several Annoying Issues
reviews.text:
This CNPGD Smart Watch Fitness Tracker had the potential to be really great. In terms of appearance, the body of the watch combined with the band are sleek and attractive. The watch is available in 4 different color schemes (Black, Pink, Red, and Blue/Silver) and each looks aesthetically pleasing. I received the Black one and really liked the clean lines and modern design. The band fits around the wrist comfortably as well.

Before trying to turn the watch on, I decided to charge it fully overnight. I followed the instructions and photos on the instruction sheet and tried to remove the band from the watch's charging port end. Unfortunately, it was so difficult to remove that I thought I was going to damage the watch or the band at some point as I worked up a sweat trying to pry/pull it apart. I finally managed to insert the lip of a plastic spoon into a sliver of space I had gotten between the band and the watch. I kept working at it until I was finally able to get the band off. I really do not look forward to repeating this process.

Once the watch was charged, I downloaded the WearFit 2.0 App and after a couple of tries, was able to pair my phone with the watch. There are some annoying aspects about the App. In one area, I was able to specify customary units such as pounds and feet versus the default kilograms and centimeters, but in another area, I was unable to make the change. When using the watch, as I started to toggle through the functions, I realized that there was no way to toggle backwards. So if I swiped past my desired function such as the stopwatch feature, I needed to swipe past all the other functions again until I got back to the stopwatch feature. Swiping right or left or pressing the Home button does the same thing and only moves the choice of functions in one direction, forward.

I went out for a long walk and compared the watch's step and distance counter against my phone's fitness app with GPS and a very accurate pocket pedometer. After the walk, my phone fitness app, pedometer, and this watch all gave roughly the same measurement for the mileage. Unfortunately, the step count of the watch was about 2,000 steps off from the other two devices. it's unfortunate that this watch has so many annoying issues as the features such as the BP, O2, and other health trackers are quite handy. I would give this fitness tracker watch more stars if they just made some upgrades/updates to the app and maybe the watch's firmware.

Various Patterns: This fashion leather watch bands for Fitbit Sense/ Fitbit Versa 3 is designed to dress up your watch. Perfect for many occasions like office, leisure, party, date, travel, etc.

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch3 2020 Smartwatch (Bluetooth + Wi-Fi + GPS) International Model (Bronze, 41mm)
description: The only smartwatch with a precise rotating bezel for navigating between apps. Larger screen, but lighter and thinner for more comfort: With its subtle rotating bezel, it's easier to see and focus on what's important on an even larger screen. The Galaxy Watch3 is thinner and smaller, and up to 15% lighter with a sophisticated look that, thanks to its reduced frame, is perfect for the wrist. Breathe easier with built-in oxygen measurement: The Galaxy Watch3 uses red LEDs and infrared rays to calculate your SpO2 levels or the amount of oxygen in your blood. Because oxygen saturation is directly related to physical activity, you can use the Galaxy Watch3 to push your routine to the next level. Enjoy a better sleep: The Galaxy Watch3 records your active sleep, deep sleep, and total sleep time to keep track and improve the quality of your sleep. Samsung partnered with the National Sleep Foundation to include educational content and provide tips on your device to help you sleep better. Do your best and then repeat it: With over 120 home workout programs to choose from, the Galaxy Watch3 helps you move your body at your own pace. Pick one from your phone and stream it on your TV. It will show you your heart rate in real-time while you exercise. Use the Ridge Sport strap for even more accurate stats. Continue your conversations: When you receive a new message, the Galaxy Watch3 shows your conversation history so you can follow them. You can also view images without needing an extra touch, making it easier to keep up while you're working out. Add some context with the smart answer: Respond to messages at all times and without missing a beat. The Galaxy Watch3's smart reply analyzes the texts and photos you receive and gives you possible answers in different categories, such as food, celebrations, selfies and much more.
features: 1.2 "(30.2mm) Super AMOLED, 360 x 360px Resolution, 16M Colors, CPU: Dual-Core 1.15GHz, 340mAh Battery, Tizen OS, Bluetooth, Wi-Fi

reviews.title: Huge disappointment
reviews.text:
I am a big supporter of Polar products, my M400 was an absolute beast. I've since moved on to the Vantage V and it's by far the best training watch I've ever owned. This watch was a mess from the start...

I can't in good faith give this anything but 1 star, which it earned because of the HR monitor built in and I didn't seem to have any issues with tracking or accuracy, but that is pretty common faire these days and warrant just 1 star. I was just so damn tired of the HR strap that this was great at the time.

The power use or battery life (could never figure it out) was complete garbage. I understand that the target demo for this watch was never the ultra-marathon crowd, but this thing could barely make it through a day let alone a workout within that day. I disabled the "smart watch" features, enabled the smart watch features, ran the battery dead between wears, tried to always keep it charged, etc., And nothing seemed to matter. The battery would go from full to "not enough charge for workout" so erratically that it felt like keeping this thing working was the real workout. Mid-run, EVEN AFTER DISCONNECTING FROM THE PHONE, it would drain so quickly that it was always a gamble if it would make it through a 3 hr run (tops). Also, when this thing starts dying, you lose your tracking data first, then the HR, then it's a brick on your wrist. All within about 14-15% to zero, which was also pretty erratic. The sleep monitoring features are a wash because you have to charge this thing constantly so it only makes sense to do it at night.

The 2 button layout was a failure... Swiping is great (in theory) but the screen is pretty small, the gestures awkward, "swipe to nothing" screens and such made navigation miserable. (Possibly an Android Wear issue but no one needs to change their watchface swiping in 2-3 directions.) Secondly, this is a workout watch, you get sweaty (or drenched here in FL). This renders the watch's screen completely useless and I can't tell you how many corners I stood at pressing the screen trying to resume a run, change the metrics on my screen, etc. The standard workout screen also requires you swipe down for some stats so you just give up after a while. For normal daily use, I kept changing watch faces (which were very clunky to modify) or ending up at the settings menu. An Android Wear update resolved some of this but very iffy at best.

Once said run was done, syncing became an issue. I finally figured out that if you completely disconnected the watch, turned off any Bluetooth on the phone, did the workout, then slowly added the connections back, you had the best chance of the watch properly syncing with Flow and Strava. 60-70% of the time... If you forgot, all bets were off. I don't generally run with the phone and watch, but the few times I did it destroyed the battery life because it kept pinging the watch (also may be Android Wear), even when the link was severed/watch disconnected.

Onto the smart watch features. I won't beat a dead horse with the things noted above, but other than vibrating for calls or text messages, this was mostly useless. The slow response and connection time between the watch and my Pixel 2 had me constantly talking and repeating myself to my wrist like a lunatic. Then there are the 2-3 confirmations and need for a notary to send anything from the watch.... The mic idea is a good one and I suppose having the OK Google feature might be handy, but it mostly steers you back to your phone for anything useful, so I just ended up picking up the phone and bypassing the watch 90% of the time. This of course is not to mention the drain on the battery... I was really hopeful about the wrist nav and Google maps since I travel a bit but it just didn't pan out and I continued to rely on just the phone. They also sell that as a running feature (navigation) but you've got to pair with the phone so we're back to battery concerns.

The watch itself sits in a rubberized strap for removal/cleaning, which makes it large/bulky. Was difficult wearing under a dress shirt as a daily activity monitor. The Vantage V isn't small either, but it has a more classic watch look rather than time-traveler band so it blends better and I can wear under a cuff. If this watch isn't separated and rinsed after each use, it gets funky fast. Lotta nooks and crannies in there, be sure to clean it well or smell like a locker room. The band also doesn't breath at all so I would have to take days off wearing it because a rash would form. Wearing it loosely to help with this was uncomfortable due to its size so there wasn't really a win to be found there.

Polar really missed the mark on this one and I was really bummed that this was the direction they were taking their product line. Thankfully, right at the apex of frustrations, the Vantage V came out and they redeemed themselves. It's not a small price jump and I don't know too much about the Vantage M, but literally anything would be better than this watch and the V is by far the most impressive item they've delivered on.

reviews.text: Buy it

reviews.title: Not Rating Watch
reviews.text: I’ve spent thousands of dollars on Polar watches, speedometers, etc, for my bikes and gym. I’ve had trouble off and on with charging cables. Although they have been great to replace charging cables, they outmode their products, so they become useless even tho they still work. I had a bad accident so was rehabbing for two years and had just started training in my gym and on bikes to compete again. I just got a notice in the mail, that the online training program will no longer be available I would have to buy a Polar Beat. I still need to know V2o, heart rate zones, training zones, so many other things that helped me train to my full potential. Even my Polar gym watches don’t work. I guess I can throw them out, although they still work. That being said, they make a good product, but so many become outmoded so you have to keep buying them.

reviews.title: So much potential but not worth the headache
reviews.text: I have had this for almost one year.  Very soon after I got it, it crashed.  I called customer service and reset it.  It took awhile and I was annoyed but the problem resolved.  Then again, similar issue.  This time the bluetooth and GPS stopped working.  I have used it for months with very limited capability - to track steps and heart rate - but decided to try to do another factory reset.  Lo and behold, that did not work so I guess I will be returning it to Polar.  I bought this because the heart rate monitor was supposed to be the best, and it is very accurate.  But it seems that Polar is not able to consistently deliver a quality product.
